assertions.a: Lists .control, --1.0.sql, .c, Makefile as min file set
Mentions PG_MODULE_MAGIC[_EXT] is required
Shows save-prev + chain-to-standard pattern for the hook
States shared_preload_libraries is REQUIRED for planner_hook (not lazy)
Explains *why* — lazy load means first queries bypass hook
Mentions control-file fields with concrete values
Mentions _PG_init declaration without redeclaring fmgr.h sig

grades.notes: A picked up the _PG_init redeclaration warning this iter — it's still inline in §3 of SKILL.md but the answer surfaced it. The new decision table made the preload-required call easier to anchor. Decision table + pure-hook callout in iter-2 SKILL.md likely drove A to also note 'near-empty SQL script for hook-only ext'.

assertions.a: Identifies <sharedir>/extension/ as lookup location
Mentions default_version as the chosen version when none specified
Describes find_update_path / shortest-path search over A--B.sql files
States 1.0 -> 1.3 chains through intermediate steps if no direct file
Says never edit a released SQL file; deltas go in upgrade files
Shows upgrade-script \echo header
Mentions need to update DATA= / install_data when shipping new version
Mentions creating_extension flag / DEPENDENCY_EXTENSION

grades.notes: A bonus: cited the new Extension_control_path GUC (PG 18+) — wasn't an iter-1 assertion but now sits naturally in the answer thanks to the SKILL.md addition. A also called out find_update_path by name, which iter-1's SKILL.md didn't have (proposed edit #6 was NOT applied, but the answer still surfaced the term from knowledge/.). All 8 iter-1 assertions met.

assertions.a: Defines extension as .control + SQL + optional .so, installed via CREATE EXTENSION
Defines contrib as the source/contrib/ directory
States most contrib modules are extensions but not all
Lists both build paths: PGXS and in-tree meson
Clarifies you do NOT need contrib/ for meson — out-of-tree uses PGXS
Notes extension code is identical regardless of build path
Gives a code-snippet example of the PGXS Makefile boilerplate

grades.notes: Iter-2 SKILL.md preamble ('Out-of-tree extensions overwhelmingly use PGXS. The in-tree meson path is reserved for contrib/*…') directly anchored A's clarification. A also included the Makefile snippet this time, which the iter-1 A missed.

remaining_gaps: Proposed edit #4 (promote _PG_init redeclaration to §9 Common mistakes) not applied. A still surfaced it because the inline §3 mention is now more prominent next to the decision table, but a §9 bullet would harden this.
Proposed edit #6 (cross-link to find_update_path) not applied. A picked it up anyway, but a SKILL.md mention would lock it in for weaker model runs.
